title: Tuberculosis (TB) and Drug Resistance
focus: Types of TB based on site of infection and drug response, and India's incidence reduction.
rationale: TB is a major public health issue in India. The distinction between MDR-TB, XDR-TB, and the affected organs (like Pott's disease) are classic UPSC targets.
sectionExcerpt: According to the WHO Global TB Report 2025, India's TB incidence reduced by 21%. TB is an airborne disease caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis. Types include Pulmonary, Extrapulmonary, and Spinal TB (Pott's disease). Drug-sensitive TB responds to isoniazid and rifampicin. MDR-TB does not respond to these two, while XDR-TB resists even stronger medicines.
evidenceBullets: India's TB incidence reduced by 21% according to the WHO Global TB Report 2025.
Spinal TB is also known as Pott's disease and affects the spine.
Multi Drug Resistant TB (MDR-TB) does not respond to rifampicin and isoniazid, the two most effective first-line TB drugs.

title: Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) and Lenacapavir
focus: HIV mechanism, transmission, and the new PrEP drug Lenacapavir.
rationale: The introduction of a new PrEP drug (Lenacapavir) and the specific biological mechanism of HIV (CD4 cells) are highly testable in Science & Tech.
sectionExcerpt: FDA approved Lenacapavir, an antiretroviral medicine used for HIV prevention as Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis (PrEP). HIV attacks the immune system by destroying CD4 T lymphocytes (white blood cells). It transmits through body fluids and from mother to baby. Treated with antiretroviral therapy (ART).
evidenceBullets: Lenacapavir is an antiretroviral medicine used as Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis (PrEP) for HIV prevention.
HIV damages the immune system by infecting and destroying CD4 cells (CD4 T lymphocytes).
HIV can be transmitted from a mother to her baby.

title: Trachoma Elimination in India
focus: India's elimination of Trachoma, its causes, and impacts.
rationale: India receiving the WHO Certificate of Elimination for Trachoma is a major milestone. Details about the pathogen and its classification as an NTD are important.
sectionExcerpt: India was awarded the WHO Certificate of Elimination of Trachoma as a Public Health Problem, becoming the 3rd SE Asian country to do so. Trachoma is an eye disease caused by Chlamydia trachomatis, categorized as a Neglected Tropical Disease (NTD), leading to irreversible blindness. Integrated under the NPCBVI.
evidenceBullets: India is the 3rd Southeast Asian country to eliminate Trachoma.
Trachoma is caused by the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis and leads to irreversible blindness.
It is classified as a Neglected Tropical Disease (NTD) by the WHO.

title: Hepatitis and Vaccine Availability
focus: Classification of Hepatitis D as cancer-causing, its dependence on Hepatitis B, and vaccine availability for different strains.
rationale: The interplay between Hepatitis B and D, the lack of a Hepatitis C vaccine, and the indigenous Havisure vaccine are prime candidates for statement-based questions.
sectionExcerpt: WHO classified Hepatitis D as cancer-causing. Hepatitis D is an incompetent virus requiring Hepatitis B virus (HBV) for replication. Only Hepatitis B, C, and D lead to chronic infections with a higher risk of liver cirrhosis or cancer. There is no vaccine for Hepatitis C. Hepatitis B vaccines protect against HDV. Havisure is India's first indigenous Hepatitis A vaccine.
evidenceBullets: Hepatitis D requires the Hepatitis B virus (HBV) for replication.
Only Hepatitis B, C, and D can lead to chronic infections risking liver cirrhosis or cancer.
There is no vaccine available for the Hepatitis C virus.
Havisure is India's first indigenous Hepatitis A vaccine.

title: Mpox (Monkeypox)
focus: Characteristics, transmission, and global status of Mpox.
rationale: Mpox's status as a PHEIC and its zoonotic nature make it relevant for current affairs in health.
sectionExcerpt: Mpox is a viral zoonotic disease caused by the Monkeypox virus (Orthopoxvirus genus). It transmits through close contact with infected entities. Symptoms include fever and swollen lymph nodes. It was declared a PHEIC by WHO, though the outbreak in Africa is no longer a global health emergency. Diagnosed via RT-PCR; no proven treatment exists.
evidenceBullets: Mpox is a viral zoonotic disease caused by a virus of the Orthopoxvirus genus.
There is no proven treatment for Mpox.
Diagnostic testing for Mpox is done using RT-PCR.

title: Human Papillomavirus (HPV) and Cervical Cancer
focus: HPV infection, its link to cervical cancer, and indigenous test kits.
rationale: The development of indigenous HPV test kits and the direct link between persistent HPV infection and cervical cancer are highly relevant for UPSC Science & Tech.
sectionExcerpt: India's first indigenously developed HPV test kits for cervical cancer screening were validated by DBT. HPV is a common STI. While the immune system usually clears it, persistent infection with high-risk HPV can cause abnormal cells leading to cancer, most commonly cervical cancer. There is currently no treatment for HPV infection.
evidenceBullets: India developed its first indigenous HPV test kits for cervical cancer screening.
Persistent infection with high-risk HPV is the most common cause of cervical cancer.
There is currently no treatment for HPV infection itself.
